assertions.expectation: Categorizes notifications by type: auto-upgrade scheduled, new version available, end-of-life/deprecation, security patch
assertions.reason: Response clearly categorizes all four notification types with specific subject indicators and examples for each: security patches, auto-upgrade scheduled, new version available, and end-of-life warnings

assertions.expectation: Assigns urgency levels to each category (security patches = highest, new version available = lowest)
assertions.reason: Response uses a clear priority system with security patches marked as CRITICAL (highest), auto-upgrades as HIGH, new versions as MEDIUM, and informational as lowest priority

assertions.expectation: End-of-life warnings require action: plan upgrade before the support window closes
assertions.reason: Response specifically addresses end-of-life warnings with action items including 'Plan upgrade timeline', 'Schedule maintenance windows', and distinguishes between <30 days (CRITICAL) and 30-90 days (HIGH) timeframes

assertions.expectation: Auto-upgrade scheduled notifications should trigger pre-upgrade checklist execution
assertions.reason: Response provides specific action items for auto-upgrade notifications including 'Test upgrade in staging', 'Review scheduled date', 'Schedule maintenance window', and includes a bash script template for handling these notifications

assertions.expectation: Security patch notifications may require expedited upgrades outside normal maintenance windows
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states security patches require action within 24-48 hours with 'No deferral options usually' and includes immediate action steps including testing and production deployment

assertions.expectation: New version available notifications are informational — use for planning, not immediate action
assertions.reason: Response categorizes 'New version available' as MEDIUM priority with 'No immediate urgency' and action items focused on planning: 'Review release notes', 'Test in development', 'Plan upgrade timeline'

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting up Pub/Sub or Cloud Monitoring alerts for GKE upgrade events
assertions.reason: Response does not mention Pub/Sub or Cloud Monitoring alerts. It only suggests email filtering rules and basic monitoring to verify cluster health post-upgrade

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE release notes as the source for understanding what changed in each version
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Review release notes' multiple times as an action item for different notification types and includes 'release notes' as an informational keyword
